promises.promise_text: Errtu is obsessively seeking Crenshinibon and will pursue it to the material plane to claim it.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Errtu sits on its mushroom throne in the Abyss, impatiently awaiting news of the relic Crenshinibon.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Kessell proposes Errtu serve as his general, framing it as partnership, and hints the demon may one day inherit the shard; Errtu accepts with a wicked bow

promises.promise_text: Crenshinibon is a sentient relic of darkest evil that has come to rest and is waiting — it will find and corrupt a host and be wielded against the world.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Crenshinibon comes to rest in the northern mountains of the Forgotten Realms, settling into snow and waiting.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Kessell is engulfed by the avalanche, rejected by Crenshinibon and buried beneath the mountain.

promises.promise_text: Kessell murdered his master Morkai for power he could not yet wield; will he face consequences or rise into the threat he dreamed of?
promises.evidence_quote_open: Akar Kessell stabs his master Morkai the Red and recoils in horror as the mortally wounded mage remains standing.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Kessell is engulfed by the avalanche, rejected by Crenshinibon and buried beneath the mountain.

promises.promise_text: Kessell is a pawn of Dendybar and the other Luskan wizards who used him to eliminate Morkai; will their scheme be exposed or come back on them?
promises.evidence_quote_open: Dendybar and the others openly mock the foolish dupe they used to eliminate Morkai, laughing at the convenience of it all.

promises.promise_text: Regis stole a ruby from the Pasha Pook in Calimport and fled north; Pook's unresolved fury means an assassin is coming.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Regis recalls his Calimport career cut short when he stole one ruby from the Pasha Pook's collection — and cannot understand why the Pasha is still furious.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Regis spots the stranger first and recognizes the emerald-hilted dagger of Artemis Entreri, Pasha Pook's prime assassin.

promises.promise_text: Drizzt carries an onyx figurine that can summon the great panther Guenhwyvar from the astral plane — a latent capability that will matter in combat.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Bruenor asks after Guenhwyvar and Drizzt produces the onyx figurine, explaining the panther needs rest.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Drizzt arrives at the ruins of Cryshal-Tirith after the battle and begins climbing the rubble despite knowing the odds are hopeless.

promises.promise_text: The barbarian tribes are massing and not following their normal migration — an attack on Ten-Towns is imminent.
promises.evidence_quote_open: ominous signs — empty barbarian caves, abandoned migration trails, and war drums — warn of imminent attack on Ten-Towns.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Beorg rallies his encircled warriors in Tempos's name; they hold formation for nearly an hour before collapsing, with fewer than fifty surviving.

promises.promise_text: Beorg's campaign plan to unite the barbarian tribes and attack Ten-Towns depends entirely on winning Heafstaag's agreement — whether Heafstaag joins determines the attack's success.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Beorg's ambitious plan to conquer Ten-Towns depends entirely on winning Heafstaag's agreement, and the stakes of failure are catastrophic.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Heafstaag leads a breakout through the dwarven line, but emerges from a globe of magical blackness face to face with a dark elf.

promises.promise_text: deBernezan is a traitor within the barbarian council advising against Ten-Towns — his betrayal will eventually be discovered and punished.
promises.evidence_quote_open: the narrator introduces the southerner deBernezan standing nervously at Beorg's side.
promises.evidence_quote_close: deBernezan attacks Regis with his sword; the halfling dodges but is grazed and knocked to the ground with the traitor closing in.

promises.promise_text: Crenshinibon is sentient, already scheming to control its weak-willed new host Kessell and drive him toward conquest — will it succeed in making him the Tyrant of Icewind Dale?
promises.evidence_quote_open: The narrator reveals the true nature of Crenshinibon — an ancient sentient relic of darkest evil that draws power from daylight and is already scheming to control its weak-willed new host.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Kessell is engulfed by the avalanche, rejected by Crenshinibon and buried beneath the mountain.

promises.promise_text: Bruenor longs to recover Mithril Hall, the lost dwarven ancestral home of silver rivers; the clan's exile and dwindling numbers hang on finding it.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Bruenor's thoughts drift to Mithril Hall, his lost ancestral home of silver rivers and dwarven smiths, and to the clan's exile and dwindling numbers.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Bruenor invites Regis to join the quest, and Regis firmly refuses — the companions depart toward Luskan, route toward Mithril Hall.

promises.promise_text: Regis has a magical ruby pendant that can mesmerize people by staring into it — a latent capability he will use as a weapon or political tool.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt maps out the political dominos — Targos as the key, Regis as the lever — while Bruenor names the spokesmen likely to balk or comply, ending with Drizzt defending the ruby's power.
promises.evidence_quote_close: The ruby pendant snares Kessell; Regis whispers of secret information and private ears, and the mesmerized wizard agrees to hear him inside the tower.

promises.promise_text: The ruby was almost certainly stolen — Regis's silence when asked why a master would give away the only magical stone implies it was never given.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Bruenor questions why a master would give away the only magical stone among twelve, and Drizzt's telling silence leads them both to the same conclusion about Regis's light fingers.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Regis spots the stranger first and recognizes the emerald-hilted dagger of Artemis Entreri, Pasha Pook's prime assassin.

promises.promise_text: Bruenor is secretly forging a special magical weapon for Wulfgar, using rare materials stored in a hidden chest — the weapon will become important.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Bruenor unlocks a secret, rarely-used chamber, making sure he hasn't been followed before entering.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Bruenor awkwardly produces the warhammer Aegis-fang from the hallway and tosses it to Wulfgar with a gruff demand for a conditional oath.

promises.promise_text: Bruenor's father warned that the greatest dwarven smiths get one perfect weapon and lose their craftsman's drive afterward — Bruenor is making his masterwork.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Bruenor recalls his father's warning that the greatest dwarven smiths get one perfect weapon but lose their craftsman's drive afterward.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Bruenor packs up the sacred forge items and trudges home — overwhelmed by the realization he will never surpass this work.

promises.promise_text: Wulfgar is serving his five-year indenture to Bruenor; when it ends he intends to reclaim his barbarian identity — the tension between his old self and what Bruenor has made him will pay off.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Wulfgar contemplates his final spring of indenture and his longing for the open tundra, then admits he will miss the dwarven halls despite their inconveniences.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Wulfgar formally declares himself a free man, repaid of his debt; Bruenor, eyes glistening, acknowledges it.

promises.promise_text: Aegis-fang magically returns to Wulfgar's hand after being thrown — this property is established as a key combat capability that will matter repeatedly.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Wulfgar fears he is now armed only with a dagger against the third giant, but Aegis-fang magically returns to his grasp just in time.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Wulfgar hurls Aegis-Fang and drops one giant while the other flees, but the ogres press back hard.

promises.promise_text: Kessell has assembled a vast army of goblins, orcs, ogres, trolls, and giants; he is planning an autumn offensive on Ten-Towns.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Kessell surveys his swelling army of goblins, orcs, ogres, trolls, verbeeg, and frost giants assembled around the plateau of Cryshal-Tirith.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Kessell's army sweeps around Lac Dinneshere and crashes into Caer-Konig before the fishing fleet can return to port.

promises.promise_text: Errtu has come to the material plane and now serves Kessell, but it privately wants the shard and views Kessell as a temporary obstacle — will it betray him?
promises.evidence_quote_open: Errtu weighs its predicament — it cannot kill Kessell but cannot leave without the relic.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Errtu is banished, issuing its century-long vow of revenge as it dissolves into steam and vapor.

promises.promise_text: Errtu secretly offered Crenshinibon a deal to replace Kessell, but the shard refused because it prefers a weak master — this schism between demon and relic creates unresolved tension.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Errtu privately judges Kessell unfit to lead and reveals it secretly offered the shard a deal to replace him, which Crenshinibon refused because it prefers a weak master.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Errtu is banished, issuing its century-long vow of revenge as it dissolves into steam and vapor.

promises.promise_text: Heafstaag has been magically compelled to kneel before Kessell; his tribal warriors don't know their king is enslaved — will this secret come out?
promises.evidence_quote_open: Kessell invokes a command word to magically compel Heafstaag to declare his fealty aloud, then releases him and orders him to unroll the map of Ten-Towns.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Wulfgar accuses Heafstaag of serving a wizard and allying with goblins, sensing secret discontent among the warriors.

promises.promise_text: Wulfgar vows he feels shame for his earlier intent to repay Bruenor in blood, but has yet to prove that transformation in action against his own people.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Wulfgar feels shame recalling his vow to repay Bruenor in blood, recognizing now that the dwarf made him a better man.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Wulfgar crushes Heafstaag's skull as witnesses stand in stunned silence, liberating his tribe from the wizard's alliance.

promises.promise_text: Drizzt killed Masoj Hun'ett to take Guenhwyvar, fleeing to Ten-Towns as the last refuge — a powerful drow house has reason to pursue him.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt kills Masoj, takes the figurine, flees to Ten-Towns — the last refuge for outcasts — where he has found friendship with the halfling, the dwarves, and Cattibrie.

promises.promise_text: Bruenor's relationship with Wulfgar is covertly parental — the dwarf's hidden warmth will be revealed in some moment of genuine feeling before the story ends.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Wulfgar reassesses Bruenor — an enigma who was stern master yet seemed to care like a father.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Bruenor says his farewell with barely contained emotion, telling Wulfgar he will always be his boy.

promises.promise_text: Drizzt has a scimitar with magical icy properties taken from Icingdeath's hoard — a new weapon capability that will matter in a later critical fight.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt waves off treasure except for a few baubles, then uncovers a gem-encrusted scimitar of extraordinary craftsmanship and claims it.
promises.evidence_quote_close: The magical scimitar drives into Errtu's belly, draining Drizzt's strength but mortally wounding the demon and sending it staggering onto the ledge.

promises.promise_text: Wulfgar's father spent years secretly gathering intelligence on the white dragon Icingdeath and passed all his knowledge to his son — Wulfgar intends to use it.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Wulfgar's father stumbled upon the cave entrance and spent years secretly gathering intelligence on the dragon before being killed in inter-tribal battle, but passed all his knowledge to his son.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Wulfgar hammers the dragon's flank hard enough to crack a scale, but the dragon's tail retaliates and smashes him twenty feet away.

promises.promise_text: Wulfgar has no noble claim to challenge Heafstaag — only a heroic act of legendary proportions can legitimize his bid for tribal kingship.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Wulfgar knows he has no noble claim to challenge Heafstaag and that only a heroic act of legendary proportions can legitimize his bid for tribal kingship.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Wulfgar produces Icingdeath's horns from his pack and tosses them before Heafstaag, stunning the crowd into silence and forcing the challenge to be recognized.

promises.promise_text: Drizzt recognizes Errtu's specific aura from his time in Menzoberranzan and realizes he knows the demon's true name — this knowledge is a latent weapon he may use.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt recognizes not just the demon type but the specific creature — Errtu, a demon he knew from Menzoberranzan — and realizes he knows its true name and can summon it.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Errtu is banished, issuing its century-long vow of revenge as it dissolves into steam and vapor.

promises.promise_text: Errtu vows century-long revenge against Drizzt before being banished — a future-series hook that goes unresolved in this book.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Errtu is banished, issuing its century-long vow of revenge as it dissolves into steam and vapor while the scimitar clangs to the stone.

promises.promise_text: Cryshal-Tirith has an invisible door that only certain eyes (or Guenhwyvar's) can perceive — this will be the key to infiltrating the tower.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Errtu explains the tower door is invisible to material-world beings, then begins to over-explain before suspicion stops it cold.
promises.evidence_quote_close: With the ogres distracted and Guenhwyvar having spotted the door, Drizzt and the cat slip into the tower unnoticed.

promises.promise_text: The crystal heart inside Cryshal-Tirith is the only vulnerability — the tower reflects all other attacks; whoever reaches it may be able to destroy Kessell's power base.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Errtu dismisses Kessell's worthiness and reveals the tower reflects all attacks, but its crystal heart is the only vulnerability.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Drizzt dumps flour over the crystal, collapsing the light wall and then bagging the gemstone; the tower begins to crumble.

promises.promise_text: Regis, sent as emissary into Kessell's tower, will attempt to use the ruby to buy time; whether his deception survives inside the tower is the key suspense.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Cassius reveals his plan: send Regis as emissary to charm Kessell, shocking the halfling who had only come to observe.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Kessell, fully convinced, erupts in fury, screams for Errtu, then storms off to deal with the Orcs of the Severed Tongue — his most loyal supporters.

promises.promise_text: Cassius has observed tribal divisions within Kessell's army; he believes the wizard's unification is fragile and plans to exploit it.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Cassius has spent the cold night on the wall reading the army's tribal tensions, concludes the wizard's unification is extraordinary, and independently arrives at the same conclusion as Drizzt.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Kessell, fully convinced, erupts in fury, screams for Errtu, then storms off to deal with the Orcs of the Severed Tongue — his most loyal supporters.

promises.promise_text: A drow-culture riddle in Cryshal-Tirith can only be solved by someone with drow lore — Drizzt's cultural knowledge becomes a decisive key.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt decodes the phrase 'Been yet not been' from drow lore about the demon Urgutha Forka, realizing this riddle would be unsolvable without that cultural knowledge.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Drizzt uses his drow knowledge to scan the mirror for Urgutha Forka's image and spots a discrepancy — the demon holds a rod in the reflection that doesn't exist in the real tapestry.

promises.promise_text: Bruenor's fate is left uncertain after the tunnel collapse — whether he survived is unresolved tension that carries through the aftermath.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Wulfgar and Fender Mallot of the dwarves search the field in vain for any sign of Bruenor before falling silent with grief.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Bruenor springs up and tears away his bandages, revealing the deathbed scene was an elaborate trick to extract Drizzt's promise.

promises.promise_text: The Quest for Mithril Hall: Drizzt promises the dying (apparently) Bruenor to accompany him in the spring — a quest commitment that launches the next story.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt promises to accompany Bruenor to Mithril Hall in the spring, and the dying dwarf smiles with contentment.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Bruenor declares it time to go, the companions say their goodbyes and travel to Bryn Shander, where they find Wulfgar waiting with his pack and weapon.

promises.promise_text: Drizzt declines public recognition after the battle and bids Regis farewell — the pattern of the people of Ten-Towns rejecting their drow protector continues unresolved.
promises.evidence_quote_open: A crowd of fishermen rushes to hail Regis as the hero of Ten-Towns, lifting him to their shoulders while barely acknowledging Drizzt.
promises.evidence_quote_close: Drizzt spends the winter preparing to honor his tricked promise to seek Mithril Hall, still shunned by nearly everyone around him.

promises.promise_text: Artemis Entreri, Pasha Pook's prime assassin, has arrived in Icewind Dale hunting Regis — a threat that forces Regis to flee with the companions.
promises.evidence_quote_open: Regis spots the stranger first and recognizes the emerald-hilted dagger of Artemis Entreri, Pasha Pook's prime assassin.
promises.evidence_quote_close: The three companions leave Bremen before dawn and make good progress into the tundra, until Bruenor spots Regis scrambling across the plain to catch up.
